Do you have a question about your Broadway show?
Does anyone actually want to see Spider-Man fly over their heads on a $75 million budget?
Is turning a 90-minute animated movie into a 3-hour musical really giving people more of what they loved?
Should we base an entire show around roller skating? On a stage?
Do audiences want to see Shrek... but live and in green face paint?
Has anyone outside this room ever described the music of Jimmy Buffett as 'theatrical'?
Should I cast a Jonas brother?
If the director and the choreographer aren't speaking to each other, should we maybe address that before tech?
Is a jukebox musical based on Cher's catalog automatically a good idea just because Cher exists?
Can we really musicalize Lestat the Vampire and expect people to take it seriously?
Has a movie-to-musical adaptation of a movie that wasn't a musical ever worked without us adding something new?
Should the fact that this is based on a Sylvester Stallone movie give us pause?
Does the world need a musical about Princess Diana? Like, need need?
If our flying rig has injured this many people in previews, is that a sign?
Should we maybe do an out-of-town tryout instead of workshopping this in front of New York critics?
If we can't explain the plot in two sentences, can an audience follow it in two acts?
Are we spending $15 million because the show requires it, or because nobody had the nerve to say no?
Would I buy a ticket to this if my name wasn't on the poster?
If the test audience describes the show as 'interesting,' should we be concerned?
Are we making this for the audience or for the Tony nominating committee?
Should I be worried that the only person who loves this concept is me and my investor's wife?
Is 'it'll find its audience' code for 'this doesn't have one'?
If Ben Brantley looks like that during intermission, should we start packing?
Should we open the week after Hamilton transfers?
Does our show need a disclaimer? Because if it needs a disclaimer...
If the merchandise team can't figure out what to put on a t-shirt, do we have a brand problem?
Should I make the show longer?
